name: Saint Paul (United States)

colors.symbolism: the upper and lower stripes and the central emblem
the central stripe and the star at the heart of the emblem

description: Three horizontal bands — yellow, blue, yellow — with a yellow stylised cross-shaped emblem spanning all three stripes at the centre and a blue five-pointed star within the emblem.
history: Saint Paul's municipal flag combines the yellow-and-blue colours of the city with a cross emblem alluding to its namesake, the apostle Paul, and a star marking its role as Minnesota's state capital.

features.symbolism: Three horizontal bands of equal height — yellow on top and bottom, blue across the middle — frame the central civic emblem.
A stylised yellow device evoking Saint Paul's namesake — the apostle Paul — bridging the upper and lower yellow stripes through the central blue band.

features.role: five-pointed star at the centre of the emblem
features.symbolism: Marks Saint Paul as the capital of Minnesota, the 'North Star State'.
